completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 100% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (100% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is published.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
findings.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
findings.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

findings.fix: Enable 'remove unused shapes' (or similar) in your export tool.
findings.what: The feed contains route shapes no trip uses.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but it bloats the feed and suggests stale export data.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 2 kinds of issue across 2 instances (0 error, 2 warning, 0 informational).

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 119 days.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Not scored yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

criteria.detail: Service data covers the next 126 days.
States wheelchair access on 100% of stops and 100% of trips.

conformance.summary: This feed earns the conformance mark: valid, current, and stating wheelchair access on nearly every stop and trip.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: If you report to the National Transit Database, your GTFS agency_id should be your five-digit NTD ID by report year 2026 (2025 for full reporters). We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this one is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data covers the next 126 days.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed looks ready for your NTD certification: published at a public URL, valid, and current. The official check is still your D-10 certification.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 1 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (Pensacola Bay City Ferry).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

top_fixes.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
top_fixes.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
top_fixes.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
top_fixes.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

top_fixes.fix: Enable 'remove unused shapes' (or similar) in your export tool.
top_fixes.what: The feed contains route shapes no trip uses.
top_fixes.why: Harmless to riders, but it bloats the feed and suggests stale export data.
